title: 11 Herbal Creams For Dermatitis
intro_desc: Herbal creams for dermatitis can be a game-changer for people who suffer from this condition. Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, is one of the most popular herbs used to treat dermatitis. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. This is because calendula has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that work to protect the skin from further damage.  Symphytum officinalal, or comfrey, is another herb that's often used to treat dermatitis. It contains allantoin, a compound that helps to promote healing and tissue repair. When applied as a cream, comfrey can help to reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms, such as itching and cracking.  Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is also a commonly used herb in dermatitis creams. It's known for its cooling and soothing properties, which can help to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Aloe vera also contains vitamins and minerals that can help to promote healing and tissue repair.  Herbal teas may not be as direct a treatment for dermatitis as creams, but they can still have a positive impact on the condition. Drinking herbal teas can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for dermatitis. When we're under stress, our immune system can become weakened, making us more prone to skin conditions like dermatitis. By drinking herbal teas, we can help to calm our minds and bodies, reducing the likelihood of flare-ups.  Herbal teas can also help to promote healing and tissue repair from the inside out. Many herbs used in teas, such as calendula and comfrey, have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Drinking herbal teas can be a gentle and holistic way to support the body's natural healing processes, which can be especially beneficial for people who are prone to dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids, including calendulin, calendulic acid, and lycopene. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. Calendula's flavonoids and phenolic acids also exhibit antimicrobial activity, preventing infection and promoting wound healing. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin is attributed to its high water content and glycosides, which help balance the skin's pH and reduce irritation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can be an effective treatment for dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Dry the Calendula flowers in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours. Let them cool completely.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and jojoba oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Add the dried Calendula flowers to the melted mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids and pour the liquid into a glass jar. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains a variety of biologically active constituents, including allantoin, vitamin C, and beta-carotene. These compounds contribute to the cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with dermatitis. Allantoin, in particular, has been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating cell proliferation and improving tissue repair. The anti-inflammatory properties of beta-carotene and vitamin C also help to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a more even skin tone. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Symphytum officinale creams can provide effective relief from the symptoms of dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Symphytum officinale leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals (10-15 seconds) until it reaches 150°F to 180°F (65°C to 82°C).
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Symphytum officinale leaves to the heated oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids. Let it cool to room temperature.

Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the cooled oil mixture. Heat it gently in a double boiler or in short intervals (10-15 seconds) until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains biologically active constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin. The aloe-emodin in Aloe barbadensis creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent cell damage, making it effective in treating dermatitis. The vitamins A and C in the cream have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing redness. The combination of these active constituents makes Aloe barbadensis creams a popular choice for treating dermatitis and promoting skin health.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from fresh Aloe barbadensis leaves. Chop leaves and squeeze gel into a bowl.
Add 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the bowl. Mix well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water. Stir until the beeswax melts.
Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool. Then, add 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and thicken. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use as needed to soothe dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita creams contains apigenin and luteolin as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce redness and irritation associated with dermatitis. Chamazulene, another key component, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that calm the skin and promote healing. The creams also contain bisabolol, a natural antiseptic that helps prevent infection and promotes tissue repair. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing, Chamomilla recutita creams provide relief from the symptoms of dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Chamomilla recutita flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Add the dried Chamomilla recutita flowers to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 2 to 3 hours, or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.

Pour the infused oil into a small saucepan and mix in 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter are melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains the bioactive constituents histamine, polysaccharides, and flavonoids. Histamine has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. Polysaccharides, particularly arabinogalactan, act as a soothing agent, calming irritated skin and promoting healing. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting skin health. By applying these bioactive compounds to the skin, Urtica dioica creams can provide relief from dermatitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil in a blender.
Blend the leaves and coconut oil together until you get a smooth paste.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the mixture and melt in a double boiler.
Pour the mixture into a tin container and let it cool and harden. Use as needed to soothe dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, glycosides, and polyphenols as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to skin inflammation. The glycosides in the cream have been found to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections that can exacerbate dermatitis. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate symptoms of dermatitis and promote skin healing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Add the shea butter and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams contains the bioactive constituents aucubin and catalpol. Aucubin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. Catalpol has antioxidant properties, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The soothing and moisturizing properties of Plantago major creams also help to calm and hydrate the skin, making it a suitable treatment for dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the bioactive constituents in Plantago major creams make it a potentially effective treatment for dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Plantago major herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago major leaves, 1 cup of olive oil, and a glass jar.
Wash the Plantago major leaves with cold water, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves and olive oil in a saucepan, heat it over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the glass jar, discard the solids.
Let the mixture cool and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks, apply it to affected skin 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains active constituents like flavonoids, naphthoquinones, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The naphthoquinones in Hypericum perforatum creams, specifically hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the cream have antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The combination of these properties makes Hypericum perforatum creams a potentially effective treatment for dermatitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add Hypericum perforatum oil and vitamin E oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the Hypericum perforatum cream in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains active constituents like cineole, linalool, and terpineol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The antiseptic properties of Melaleuca alternifolia creams also help to prevent infection and promote healing of the skin. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, which can help to calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort. When applied topically, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Add 1 cup of distilled water to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Add shea butter and stir until fully incorporated.
Add Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains tannins and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin. The tannins in Melissa officinalis creams can reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis, while the flavonoids have been shown to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis creams also help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate dermatitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Melissa officinalis creams can help to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water and add the Melissa officinalis flowers. Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl and discard the flowers. Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in the saucepan until the beeswax and coconut oil are melted. Let it cool for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Apply to affected skin 2-3 times a day to help soothe dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by dermatitis. The saponins in Saponaria officinalis creams have a gentle cleansing effect, removing dirt and bacteria from the skin without stripping it of its natural oils. The flavonoids and phenolic acids have a strong antioxidant effect, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting wound healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Saponaria officinalis creams can provide relief from the symptoms of dermatitis, such as redness, itching, and dryness.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal creams for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Saponaria officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the Saponaria officinalis flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and add the beeswax. Heat until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Use as a cream for dermatitis relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent dermatitis from forming. Certain herbs like chamomile, calendula, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin. They may also help reduce redness and irritation associated with dermatitis. Drinking these teas regularly might support healthy skin and reduce the risk of dermatitis.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your dermatitis treatment, but it's essential to use them carefully. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and calendula, have anti-inflammatory properties that may soothe skin irritations. However, consuming them daily can cause interactions with medications or worsen underlying conditions. Use them in moderation and monitor your skin's response.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can take a few days to a few weeks to show results in dermatitis. Some people notice improvement within a week, while others may need a few weeks to see a difference. It really depends on the individual and the specific herbal tea being used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for dermatitis can be most effective in the morning. This allows the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties to work throughout the day, helping to soothe and calm the skin. As the body absorbs the nutrients, it can better manage inflammation and promote healing.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for dermatitis. Some herbal teas may slow down or speed up the way these medications work. For example, if you take a medication to treat eczema and drink a tea that also slows down blood flow, it could worsen your skin condition.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a soothing choice for skin issues like dermatitis. However, it's best to be cautious when pregnant. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are considered safe in small amounts. But others, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, may not be suitable due to potential effects on the baby. Always check the ingredients and amounts carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and dealing with dermatitis, some herbal teas may be helpful, but it's essential to be cautious. Certain herbs, like chamomile and calendula, are often considered safe for breastfeeding mothers. However, others, such as pennyroyal and sage, may not be suitable due to their potential effects on milk production or baby's health.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams to Treat Dermatitis in Babies
